title: Consume a wrapper with IntoIterator
scenario: When callers are done with a wrapper, IntoIterator should let them move owned values out naturally.
instructions: The starter already has borrowed iteration with iter. Implement IntoIterator for OrderLines, yielding owned OrderLine values.

hints.body: Implement IntoIterator for OrderLines, not for &OrderLines yet.
Use std::vec::IntoIter<OrderLine> and self.lines.into_iter() because this implementation consumes the wrapper.
One complete solution for Consume a wrapper with IntoIterator is shown below.

completionExplanation: Owned IntoIterator lets callers move OrderLine values out when they are done with the wrapper, while borrowed iter remains available.
